working_definition: A dermal fibroblast in the hair-bearing skin organoid (SkO) defined by expression of periostin (POSTN), an extracellular matrix protein associated with activated fibroblast states. Present in the SkO dermal compartment; fibroblasts in SkO retain a prenatal-like rather than adult identity even after 19 weeks of culture.

best_match.cl_definition: Any skin fibroblast that is part of some dermis.

justification: CL:0002551 (fibroblast of dermis) is the closest available term. No CL term exists for a POSTN-expressing or organoid-specific dermal fibroblast. Although periostin is associated with myofibroblast-like activation in some contexts, the atlas characterises this population as fibroblasts in the SkO dermal compartment without designating them as myofibroblasts, so CL:0000186 (myofibroblast cell) is not appropriate.

other_candidates.cl_definition: A connective tissue cell which secretes an extracellular matrix rich in collagen and other macromolecules.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: Too general; no skin or dermis specificity.
Less specific than fibroblast of dermis; CL:0002551 better captures the dermal compartment location.

other_candidates.cl_definition: An animal cell that has characteristics of both a fibroblast cell and a smooth muscle cell.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: The atlas does not designate POSTN+ cells as myofibroblasts. POSTN expression alone is insufficient for myofibroblast classification, which requires alpha-smooth muscle actin expression and contractile characteristics not reported for this population.

recommendation: Use CL:0002551 (fibroblast of dermis) as a broad match. A new CL term for a POSTN+ dermal fibroblast may be warranted in future if cross-dataset evidence confirms a distinct activated fibroblast state marked by periostin in human fetal skin or organoids.
